# DAIV

> Open-source async software engineering agent for GitLab and GitHub.

DAIV turns issues into working code. You create an issue, DAIV analyzes your codebase, posts an implementation plan, and after approval, opens a merge request with the changes. If reviewers request changes, mention DAIV in a comment and it applies the fix.

- License: Apache 2.0
- Source: https://github.com/srtab/daiv
- Documentation: https://srtab.github.io/daiv/latest/
- Documentation for LLMs: https://srtab.github.io/daiv/latest/llms.txt

## Capabilities

- Issue-to-merge-request automation with human-in-the-loop approval
- Slash commands and custom skills invoked from issues and merge requests
- Secure sandboxed code execution in isolated Docker containers
- MCP (Model Context Protocol) tool integrations for external services
- Specialized subagents for exploration and execution
- Multi-provider LLM support (OpenRouter, Anthropic, OpenAI, Google Gemini)
- Jobs API for programmatic agent triggering and CI/CD integration
- Per-repository configuration and custom skill authoring

## How It Works

1. You create an issue describing what you need
2. DAIV analyzes the codebase and posts an implementation plan as a comment
3. You review and approve the plan
4. DAIV implements the changes and opens a merge request
5. Reviewers can request changes by mentioning DAIV in review comments
